start: ^Bartek: ...Did a mushroom just flirt with me?
^Kopatych: Honey, after three rainy nights and a suspicious amount of spores, anything can happen.
^Bartek: The fisherman says you stole his lucky fish.
^Kopatych: Stole is such an emotionally expensive word.
^Kopatych: Living its best life near the old pier.
^Kopatych: It looked at me with the eyes of a creature that had seen taxation.
^How do I get into your house?
fish_reason: ^Kopatych: I needed it for an experiment.
^Kopatych: The kind that becomes illegal only after someone writes it down.
^Kopatych: The fish objected to the methodology.
^Bartek: I am going to catch it.
house: ^Bartek: I need to reach the caves beneath your house.
^Bartek: Can you give me the key?
^Kopatych: That depends on your relationship with radioactive mushrooms.
insane: ^Bartek: Tell me how to reach the caves.
^Kopatych: Through the basement of my house.
quest_offer: ^Kopatych: I need four radioactive mushrooms from the glowing grove.
^Kopatych: Science. Crime. Dinner. Depends who asks.
^Kopatych: I give you the key to my house.
^Bartek: The house with the cave entrance in the basement?
^Bartek: Why is there a cave entrance under your house?
waiting_for_mushrooms: ^Kopatych: Wonderful. Short-term memory is very attractive.
deliver_mushrooms: ^Bartek: I brought your four radioactive mushrooms.
^Kopatych: They are glowing with emotional instability.
^Kopatych: As promised, here is the key to my house.
^Bartek: And the basement leads into the caves?
^Bartek: You have a guard in your basement?
^Kopatych: Technically, the guard has me above his cave.
^Kopatych: Not unless you bring something he respects.
^Bartek: You could have told me this before.
^Kopatych: Then our relationship would have developed too quickly.
after_key: ^Bartek: And the guard is waiting below.
^Kopatych: That is what makes him professional.

start: ^Fisherman: The water was looking at me again today.
^Bartek: Water does not look at people.
^Fisherman: That's exactly what it wants you to think.
^Fisherman: It has also taken my lucky fish.
^Fisherman: Then he came back alone and said she had chosen freedom.
^Fisherman: It makes betrayal easier to organize.
^What is so special about this fish?
find_kopatych: ^Fisherman: Large mushroom. Suspicious confidence. Smells like soup and bad decisions.
^Fisherman: Ask him what he did with my fish.
^Fisherman: And do not let him change the subject to chemistry.
^Fisherman: Because crime sounds more respectable when he calls it chemistry.
special_fish: ^Fisherman: She is a swamp lantern carp.
^Fisherman: Green in daylight. Golden beneath the moon.
^Fisherman: She brought me luck for seven years.
^Bartek: That is a very low standard.
^Fisherman: Find Kopatych. He knows where she is.
personal_problem: ^Bartek: That does not make it my problem.
^Fisherman: Kopatych lives directly on the road you are walking toward.
^Fisherman: Destiny is very lazy around here.
^Bartek: Fine. I will speak to him.
^Fisherman: Tell him I am still emotionally armed.
waiting_for_fish: ^Fisherman: Did Kopatych tell you where she is?
^Bartek: He released her near the old pier.
^Fisherman: He thinks freedom means creating problems for other people.
^Bartek: I still have to catch her.
^Fisherman: Then take a rod and be patient.
^Fisherman: Lucky fish do not bite for people in a hurry.
green_fish: ^Fisherman: If she chose the lake, the lake may keep her.
^Bartek: So I caught her for nothing?
^Fisherman: You brought back something I thought was gone.
^Fisherman: The old lakekeepers carried them before the caves were sealed.
^Bartek: What am I supposed to do with it?
^Fisherman: Someone below Kopatych's house may still recognize it.
^Bartek: You could have mentioned the person living under his house earlier.
^Fisherman: You had not earned the confusing part yet.
after_fish: ^Fisherman: I have been sitting here for seven years.
^Fisherman: My legs have started developing political opinions.

start: ^Bartek: I need to enter the cave.
^Guard: No one enters the lower lake.
^Guard: Kopatych once gave a key to a chicken.
^Guard: Proof that the lake has accepted you.
show_token: ^Guard: It belonged to the old lakekeepers.
^Guard: Only someone who returned what was lost may carry one.
^Guard: The lower lake remembers every visitor.
^Bartek: Does anything around here behave normally?
threaten: ^Bartek: Move, or I will make you move.
^Guard: I have guarded this door for thirty-two years.
^Guard: I have survived floods, spores, slimes and Kopatych's cooking.
^Guard: You are currently fifth on my list of concerns.
^Bartek: Fine. I will find another way.
after_open: ^Guard: Do not follow voices coming from beneath the water.
^Bartek: What if they know the way?
^Guard: Then they do not need your help.
